content.claim: No bright-line statutory minimum enrollment threshold exists in current Hungarian education law (unlike comparative models such as Ukraine's 45-student rule); closure and reorganization decisions appear to operate on a case-by-case basis determined by the maintainer/tankerület/ministry rather than automatic numerical triggers.
content.context: Hungary's statutory framework grants maintainers broad discretion to close or reorganize rural primary schools, but multiple procedural safeguards (annual implementation deadlines, mandatory consultation, phase-out options, maintainer-change alternatives, and political-legal caution) collectively slow the speed and frequency of closures below what legal authority alone would permit.
limitations: The v1 source string was not re-verified during migration.
content.population: Population described in the migrated claim
content.time_scope: As reported in the v1 source corpus
